title: How do you perceive the point of NGE
display_title: How do you perceive the point of NGE
wikitext:
[]: How do you perceive the point of NGE?             Quote Post by Recall » Mon Nov 10, 2008 2:38 pm The series is a world of fantasy Shinji created to escape from his reality. When he realised his fantasy was no different to reality, a place where he still didn't "exist" he realised what he must change to live in reality. Original episodes 25 and 26 and the final 25 minutes of EOE even spell it out for you, spoon feed you the answer. So why do people go on about the mecha so much? Did people take the mecha too literal?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 10, 2008 4:04 pm So you're saying the entire series is all in Shinji's head? That's an interesting notion, but I don't see how that jives with the finale. If that were the case I would expect the final scene to involve non-animated footage.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 10, 2008 5:03 pm Instrumentality occurred in both the series and movie ending but was rejected. Also near the end of both endings, Shinji is told that the "world of reality isn't so bad" and that "reality comes after his dream". Reality is what happened all throughout the series before Instrumentality occurs and is rejected. Too long; didn't read: The series is NOT all in Shinji's head, that's just some BS spouted by either a fan who doesn't know what they're talking about, or ADV/Mangle.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 10, 2008 5:41 pm http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/WMG/NeonGenesisEvangelion You may now all cry.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:05 pm Stuff like that makes me feel good about some of my worst moments. TVTropes should just be forbidden from writing about NGE or something. I've encountered people who'd seen the series once and grasped the concepts better.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:39 pm it's a psychodrama that happens to use robots

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:52 pm Reichu wrote: Stuff like that makes me feel good about some of my worst moments. TVTropes should just be forbidden from writing about NGE or something. I've encountered people who'd seen the series once and grasped the concepts better.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:57 pm You know, the whole main point of Eva was Shinji's penis envy of his mother. Or, at least, that's what I got out of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:59 pm At least that's set in this section of the site that's complete fanwank but yeah. Crazy theories are starting to get annoying.

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Mon Nov 10, 2008 8:13 pm ......then he woke up, and realized it was all just a dream. What a super ending!!!!!!!! Excellent theory...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 10, 2008 9:33 pm I think it's crazy too, but there's no need to be condescending to new posters as they work out the misinformation they've absorbed. At least offer some sort of argument instead of dropping a "you're wrong, blah!" post. For example, if the show was all in Shinji's head how can the various plots of which he has no knowledge (Kaji's investigations, Misato and Asuka's past, his month-long lcl vacation, Gendo's meetings with Seele and the JSSDF...and plenty of others) be explained?

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Mon Nov 10, 2008 9:47 pm I wasn't looking to being condescending to the OP, but rather to the theory on that silly website. Basically I was saying its pretty cliche and it could work in basically any series or movie, thus shitting it up immensely. Sorry if I came off that way, I just don't like the theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Nov 10, 2008 11:29 pm actually this is all just a figment of my imagination. ..... or is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 11, 2008 12:29 am Formless One wrote: Uh, aren't they trying to be funny/rediculous?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 11, 2008 1:32 am They're intentionally throwing out misinterpretations, Reichu. Funniness over Accuracy is the main rule of the entire website.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Tue Nov 11, 2008 1:36 am Oddly enough, in order to misrepresent the show on that kind of scale must mean that they actually understand the show juuuuust fine. You know how real newbs react to the show? Its not usually an informed reaction, but it is certainly not usually outright nuts .

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Nov 11, 2008 9:13 am @ Recall: I think you missed the point of EoE. Then, this isn't reality... Because no one is here. And: Rei: If you wish once more for the existence of others, the barriers of the heart will separate everyone once more... And the fear of other people will begin again. Shinji: That's all right... (disengages Rei from him and clasps her hand) Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by Recall » Tue Nov 11, 2008 11:08 am So it can't be in no shape or form just be a personification of emotions and beliefs, rather than taken so literal. Oh well, my mistake for not knowing the correct answer. Maybe I just liked the line by Rei "Its just a dream, you're using fantasy to escape reality" too much then

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Nov 11, 2008 2:55 pm @recall: No, that's exactly what happened... for those moments of the alternate worlds that Shinji visited. You're just either applying the message too broadly or confusing the message of the film intended for the audience with the in-story plot.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 11, 2008 3:03 pm Recall: The world is real, except in the ending(s), where we see worlds within Shinji's mind.
